audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar daughters-of-Zelophehad and Joshua-commissioning text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for women's inheritance petition, legal expansion, kinship succession order, Moses viewing the land, Meribah accountability, shepherd leadership, Joshua's spirit, public commissioning, and Eleazar/Urim consultation.

verses.restored: Daughters of Zelophehad son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, from clans of Manasseh son of Joseph, came near. These were names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
They stood before Moses and before Eleazar the priest and before leaders and all congregation at entrance of tent of meeting, saying,
Our father died in wilderness. He was not among congregation gathering against YHWH in congregation of Korah, but in his own sin/misalignment he died, and he had no sons.
Why should name of our father be diminished from midst of his clan because he had no son? Give us a possession among brothers of our father.
Moses brought their judgment/case near before YHWH.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Daughters of Zelophehad speak rightly. You shall surely give them possession of inheritance among brothers of their father, and you shall cause inheritance of their father to pass to them.
To children of Israel you shall speak, saying: When a man dies and has no son, you shall cause his inheritance to pass to his daughter.
If he has no daughter, you shall give his inheritance to his brothers.
If he has no brothers, you shall give his inheritance to brothers of his father.

If his father has no brothers, you shall give his inheritance to his nearest kin from his clan, and he shall possess it. It shall be for children of Israel as statute of judgment, as YHWH commanded Moses.
YHWH said to Moses, Go up to this mountain of Abarim and see land that I have given to children of Israel.
When you have seen it, you also shall be gathered to your people, as Aaron your brother was gathered,

because in wilderness of Zin, in contention of congregation, you rebelled against my mouth/command to sanctify me at waters before their eyes. These are waters of Meribah of Kadesh in wilderness of Zin.
Moses spoke to YHWH, saying,
Let YHWH, Divine of spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over congregation,
who will go out before them and come in before them, who will bring them out and bring them in, so congregation of YHWH will not be like sheep who have no shepherd.
YHWH said to Moses, Take for yourself Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom is spirit, and lay your hand upon him.
Stand him before Eleazar the priest and before all congregation, and commission him before their eyes.
You shall put from your authority/splendor upon him, so all congregation of children of Israel will listen.

He shall stand before Eleazar the priest, who shall inquire for him by judgment of Urim before YHWH. At his mouth they shall go out, and at his mouth they shall come in—he and all children of Israel with him, all congregation.
Moses did as YHWH commanded him; he took Joshua and stood him before Eleazar the priest and before all congregation.
He laid his hands upon him and commissioned him, as YHWH spoke by hand of Moses.

translation_notes: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah speak publicly before the highest communal authorities, and YHWH says their claim is right.
The case changes general inheritance law. Torah is shown responding to a marginalized petition rather than treating existing custom as untouchable.
The resulting law remains patrilineal and incomplete by modern gender-equality standards, but it materially expands daughters' rights within its setting.
Moses' leadership does not become hereditary. Joshua is selected for spirit, public service, and communal need rather than bloodline.
Joshua receives some of Moses' authority and remains accountable to Eleazar's inquiry; succession is distributed rather than absolute.
Moses' Meribah consequence remains attached to leadership accountability, not cancellation of his life's service.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 27 to deny women property, voice, leadership, or legal standing. The chapter explicitly validates women who challenge exclusion and portrays faithful law as capable of correction and expansion. Leadership succession must not become dynasty, personality cult, or immunity from accountability.
